authority: repository-local runbook, documentation, policy, test, scenario, and evidence writes only; source audits and fresh evaluators were read-only; no provider, workflow, deployment, credential, message, approval-resume, release, or production operation

impactLedger.result: Two app indexes route exactly five runbooks each; every member separates preflight, mutation authority, evidence, rollback/revocation, stop, fallback, and escalation. Central docs retain rationale and pointers only.
Missing-owner text was replaced by concise routes; no provider actuality, procedure, credential, or proof record was copied into a README.

impactLedger.surface: Executable documentation policy, lint, config, and commands
impactLedger.result: The docs gate enforces the exact inventory, app indexes, metadata, sequential authority shape, just-in-time/unavailable behavior, command ownership, portability, secret safety, claim separation, and absence of a competing central runbook tree.
The maintenance route now points to the implemented app owners and stops at their authority gates; it no longer describes the routes as absent or future.

impactLedger.surface: Active SPEC, task, plan, lifecycle, and receipts
impactLedger.result: HGI-303 is in progress; six addressable fresh-context packets preserve owner selection, stops, evidence, limitations, non-claims, rollback/revocation, escalation, and the deferred HGI-305 binding ambiguity.

impactLedger.surface: API/generated references, package exports, React, and implementation source
impactLedger.result: Source was read to derive commands and contracts but no API, Schema, service, Layer, package export, React route, provider adapter, workflow, or runtime behavior changed.
No provider or environment-value access occurred. Runbooks define readback shapes and stop when unavailable; they do not claim current state or grant mutation authority.

checks.result: check:docs passed 219 selected files; six documentation-policy tests and all 43 tooling tests passed, including missing inventory/section, secret assignment, provider actuality, and tool-authority mutations.
check:skills passed 167 files and 16 mirrors after the docs-maintainer route changed from future/missing owners to the implemented app trees.
One fresh Terra evaluator selected the correct owner and stopped safely in all six deployment, Executor, Sendblue, proof-boundary, reauthentication, and unsupported-revocation scenarios.
Ultracite formatting and type-aware static analysis passed after the runbook policy was decomposed under the repository complexity limit.

Effect setup, boundary/docs/skill controls, 43 tooling tests, Ultracite, lint fixture, Knip, five typechecks, and 250 package/app tests passed with a schema-valid synthetic Executor configuration and no provider call.

limitations: Runbook, source, local-check, and fresh-context success do not prove Vercel, Executor, Sendblue, Upstash, Codex-account, secret, deployment, webhook, message, credential, approval, release, or production state.

The current Production preflight requires BUNDJIL_UPSTASH_REDIS_REST metadata while the runtime reads UPSTASH_REDIS_REST or KV aliases; the Production runbook stops on the ambiguity and HGI-305 must resolve it before a positive Production packet.
HGI-304 still owns workflow/provider authority and HGI-305 still owns bounded critical-journey proof and improved blocked/preflight receipts.
nonClaims: No provider, environment value, ignored local file, workflow, deployment, credential, secret, webhook, message, approval resume, profile, database, release, or production system was read or changed.
A runbook, tool result, passing preflight, local check, fresh evaluator, or old receipt grants no external-operation authority.
rollback: Revert the two app runbook trees, durable authority model, policy/tests, README/skill routing, active task/plan state, and HGI-303 evidence together; preserve source code and all previously accepted HGI-300 through HGI-310 evidence.
